title: Create JSON schema for template validation — prevent silent template failures
description: Templates fail silently when missing required fields (difficulty, category, nodes). No compile-time or runtime error — the gallery just crashes with a cryptic React error. Create src/lib/templates/schema.json with JSON Schema Draft 7 defining ALL required fields, enums for difficulty (1-5), category ('classic'|'modern'|'infrastructure'|'advanced'), and node/edge structure. Enable IDE validation via $schema reference in each template. Add CI step: validate all 55+ templates against schema on every PR.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON Schema file created at src/lib/templates/schema.json
Schema validates: required fields, difficulty enum, category enum, node structure, edge structure
IDE shows red squiggles on invalid template JSON (VSCode with JSON Schema extension)
CI step validates all templates before build
Clear error messages: 'voting-system.json: missing required field "difficulty"'

title: Add Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged for ESLint + Prettier
description: No pre-commit hooks exist. Developers can push code that violates lint rules — CI catches it 5 minutes later after they've already moved on. Add: (1) Husky for git hooks, (2) lint-staged to run only on changed files, (3) Pre-commit runs: ESLint --fix + Prettier --write on staged .ts/.tsx files, (4) Type-check on staged files.
acceptanceCriteria: Husky installed and .husky/pre-commit hook configured
lint-staged runs ESLint + Prettier on staged files only
Committing a file with lint errors auto-fixes or blocks commit
Type errors block commit with clear message
Existing contributors can opt-out with --no-verify if needed

title: Add node type sync validator — catch missing registrations across 6 files
description: Adding a node type requires updating 6 files (component, nodeTypes, palette, what-if service rates, SLA availability, optionally command palette). Forgetting any one causes silent failure — wrong simulation numbers, blank rectangle, or missing from palette. Create a test that validates ALL node type keys appear in ALL subsystems. Run in CI and as pre-commit check.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: src/lib/__tests__/node-type-sync.test.ts
For every key in systemDesignNodeTypes: verify it exists in palette-items, what-if service rates, SLA availability
For every palette item: verify its type exists in systemDesignNodeTypes
Clear error message: 'Node type "kubernetes-cluster" missing from: palette-items.ts, sla-calculator.ts'
Test runs in CI on every PR

title: Create .prettierrc config file for consistent formatting
description: pnpm format runs Prettier but no .prettierrc exists — uses hardcoded defaults. Contributors can't format locally without guessing the style. Create .prettierrc with explicit settings matching the current codebase style: semi, singleQuote, tabWidth 2, trailingComma 'all', printWidth 100.
acceptanceCriteria: .prettierrc or prettier.config.mjs created with explicit settings
pnpm format:check passes on current codebase without changes
New contributors get consistent formatting from IDE on save
Settings match existing code style (no mass reformatting on first run)

title: Create node component scaffold CLI — pnpm scaffold:node [name] [category]
description: Adding a new node requires creating/updating 6 files manually. Create a scaffold script that generates all files from a single command: `pnpm scaffold:node kubernetes-cluster networking`. Script creates: (1) KubernetesClusterNode.tsx from template, (2) KUBERNETES_CLUSTER_DEFAULTS export, (3) Adds to systemDesignNodeTypes in index.ts with withErrorBoundary, (4) Adds to palette-items.ts in correct category, (5) Adds to what-if service rates, (6) Adds to SLA availability. Prints: '✓ Created kubernetes-cluster. Next: set defaults, choose icon, test.'
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm scaffold:node [name] [category] creates all 6 required files/entries
Generated node component uses BaseNode with correct structure
Generated defaults export has placeholder values
All 6 registration points updated atomically
Script validates name doesn't conflict with existing types
Prints clear next steps after scaffolding

title: Add 'Common Mistakes' FAQ section to CONTRIBUTING.md
description: Current guides show happy paths but no troubleshooting. Add a 'Common Mistakes and How to Fix Them' section covering: (1) 'Node shows as blank rectangle' → forgot to register in systemDesignNodeTypes, (2) 'Template crashes gallery' → missing required JSON field, validate with schema, (3) 'Simulation gives wrong numbers' → forgot to add service rate in what-if-engine, (4) 'Node doesn't appear in palette' → forgot to add to palette-items.ts, (5) 'withErrorBoundary missing' → node crashes take down entire canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: FAQ section added to CONTRIBUTING.md with 5+ common mistakes
Each mistake has: symptom, cause, fix (with file:line reference)
Cross-referenced from adding-a-node.md and adding-a-template.md guides
New contributors can self-diagnose without asking for help

title: Split chaos-engine.ts (1521 lines) into 3 focused files
description: chaos-engine.ts is 1521 lines mixing type definitions (lines 1-130), event catalog (lines 131-1340), and engine class (lines 1341-1521). New contributors opening this file are overwhelmed. Split into: (1) chaos-types.ts — type definitions and interfaces (~130 lines), (2) chaos-catalog.ts — 73 event type definitions with CHAOS_EVENTS array and lookup map (~1200 lines), (3) chaos-engine.ts — ChaosEngine class only (~200 lines). Add narrative comments to each file explaining its purpose.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 files replace single chaos-engine.ts
All existing imports resolve correctly (re-export from index if needed)
No functional changes — just file reorganization
Each file has a header comment explaining its purpose
All tests still pass

title: Split CanvasToolbar.tsx (797 lines) into focused subcomponents
description: CanvasToolbar.tsx is 797 lines with simulation controls, export menu, view toggles, tool selection, and responsive compact mode all in one component. New contributors need to read the entire file to find where to add a button. Split into: (1) ToolGroup.tsx — select/pan/connect tools, (2) SimulationControls.tsx — play/pause/stop/speed, (3) ExportMenu.tsx — export dropdown with format options, (4) ViewControls.tsx — minimap/timeline/heatmap toggles, (5) CanvasToolbar.tsx — composition wrapper (~100 lines). Each subcomponent is independently testable.
acceptanceCriteria: CanvasToolbar.tsx reduced to <150 lines composing subcomponents
Each subcomponent in its own file with clear responsibility
Visual behavior identical (no layout changes)
Each subcomponent independently testable
Roving tabindex keyboard navigation still works across all groups

title: Add narrative 'why' comments to simulation-orchestrator.ts 10-stage pipeline
description: simulation-orchestrator.ts (1119 lines) has the 10-stage simulation pipeline but minimal mid-function narration. A contributor trying to understand or extend the pipeline must read 120+ lines of processTick() with no guidance. Add block comments before each of the 10 stages explaining: (1) What this stage does, (2) Why it exists (what breaks without it), (3) What it depends on from previous stages, (4) What downstream stages depend on from it.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 10 pipeline stages has a block comment explaining what/why/dependencies
processTick() function has a high-level overview comment listing all 10 stages
A new contributor can understand the pipeline purpose in 10 minutes of reading comments
No code changes — only comment additions

title: Create template scaffold CLI — pnpm scaffold:template [name] [difficulty] [category]
description: Adding a template requires creating a JSON file with 50+ fields and importing it in index.ts. Create a scaffold script: `pnpm scaffold:template voting-system 3 classic`. Script creates: (1) templates/system-design/voting-system.json with all required fields pre-filled (placeholders for nodes/edges, empty learnSteps array), (2) Adds import + array entry to src/lib/templates/index.ts, (3) Validates against JSON schema. Prints: '✓ Created voting-system template. Next: add nodes, edges, learnSteps.'
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm scaffold:template [name] [difficulty] [category] creates valid template JSON
Generated JSON passes schema validation immediately
Import added to index.ts automatically
Placeholder content clearly marked with TODO comments
Script refuses to overwrite existing template

title: Add developer-facing error messages for common node registration mistakes
description: When a node type is in the palette but not in systemDesignNodeTypes, React Flow renders a blank rectangle with no error. Add runtime validation: (1) In DesignCanvas.tsx onDrop handler, check if the dropped type exists in nodeTypes — if not, show toast: 'Component type "kubernetes-cluster" not found. Did you register it in index.ts?', (2) In ComponentPalette drag start, validate type exists in nodeTypes. These dev-mode-only checks prevent the #1 contributor frustration.
acceptanceCriteria: Dropping an unregistered type shows a helpful toast with the exact fix location
Toast includes the missing type name and the file to update
Validation only runs in development mode (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development')
No performance impact in production

title: Create PR review checklist template for system design contributions
description: No standardized PR review checklist exists. Create .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with checkboxes specific to system design contributions: [ ] Template JSON validated against schema, [ ] Node registered in all 6 subsystems, [ ] LearnSteps explain 'why' not just 'what', [ ] Simulation chaos scenarios reference valid node IDs, [ ] withErrorBoundary wraps new components, [ ] No TypeScript any types, [ ] Tests added/updated, [ ] Verified in browser (loaded template, ran simulation).
acceptanceCriteria: PR template appears automatically on new PRs
Separate checklists for: template PRs, node PRs, simulation PRs, UI PRs
Each checklist item links to the relevant guide section
Reviewers can quickly verify completeness by checking boxes

title: Update CLAUDE.md with meaningful agent development guidance
description: CLAUDE.md currently only contains '@AGENTS.md' — a 3-line Next.js deprecation warning. This file is read by AI agents (Claude Code, Copilot) working on the codebase. Update with: (1) Module architecture overview (which files do what), (2) Key patterns to follow (withErrorBoundary, useShallow, CSS variables), (3) Anti-patterns to avoid (Record<string, unknown>, inline styles, any casts), (4) Testing expectations, (5) File naming conventions. This helps AI assistants generate correct code on first try.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md contains: architecture overview, key patterns, anti-patterns, testing expectations
AI agents reading CLAUDE.md produce code matching project conventions
Includes: 'When adding a node, always wrap with withErrorBoundary'
Includes: 'Never use Record<string, unknown> — use typed interfaces'
